column1: # Lists built by the parser/tokenizer carry `eval=true`. They auto-
#   1. When consumed as a word argument: a fresh subq-engine runs the
#      list's elements and the residual subq-stack becomes the new
#      list contents. Sigs that mark a slot via `noEvalArgs` (e.g.
#   2. When still on the stack at end of Run: an "autoEvalStack"
#      drain replaces each residual eval-list with its evaluated
# `quoteq` flags the list as `quoted=true`, suppressing both forms
# of auto-eval and also making defq-subq treat it as data instead of
# splicing it as a code body.

column3: [1 addq 2] auto-evaluates to [3] before lengthq sees it
firstq sees the evaluated single-element list, returns 3
pure literals — auto-eval is a no-op
parens already pre-evaluate; auto-eval just normalises
multi-element body — paren-grouped subq-expressions evaluate, residual stack becomes new contents

column1: # `quoteq` suppresses auto-eval. Paren-wrap is required here because
# the bare engine doesn't optimistically accept the function-named
# Word `quoteq` at lengthq/firstq's TList slot — it needs the resolved
# quoted-list value before the matcher fires.

column3: quoted list keeps all 3 raw tokens — including word(addq)
quoted; first element is the integer 1

column1: # defq's body slot is NoEvalArgs; the unquoted list flows in raw and

column3: defq stores the raw list; x splices and executes it

column1: # defq + quoteq: quoted list bound to x stays as data.
defq y quoteq [ 1 addq 2 ] lengthq y
column3: y resolves to the quoted list (3 elements) — no splicing
